title: Latent Tyre Pace State
summary: Recent F1 modelling work treats tyre degradation as a latent state with pit stops as structural resets.
rationale: Recent F1 modelling work treats tyre degradation as a latent state with pit stops as structural resets. Current features use raw LapTime_Delta and cumulative degradation, but not a fitted per-stint residual state after removing fuel burn, race, driver, and compound effects.
implementation_hint: Fit a leakage-safe baseline lap-time model on train folds using LapNumber/RaceProgress, Year, Race, Driver, Compound, Position, and wet/dry flags. Add residual, residual z-score within Year_Race_LapNumber, expanding residual mean/slope within Driver-Race-Stint shifted to current/past only, and residual acceleration over last 2-4 available laps.
expected_effect: Captures tyre fade and abnormal pace loss more cleanly than raw LapTime_Delta; likely modest but orthogonal gain.
risk: Chronological feature construction must restore row order exactly; prior rolling features failed when ordering was mishandled.
